title: Finland Earned Income and Deductions (50A), Form 3023e
description: The Finnish Tax Administration's (Verohallinto, "Vero") form 50A, "Earned income and deductions" (Finnish: "Ansiotulot ja niihin kohdistuvat vähennykset", internal reference 3023e), used to report earned income (wages and salary, pension income, benefits, and other earned income) missing from an individual's pre-completed (prefilled) annual tax return, or to correct information already on it. The form also covers related deductions (union/unemployment-fund dues, work-related expenses, donations, a reduction in the taxable value of an employer-provided car benefit) and child support payments, plus relationship-status changes and cross-border/non-resident tax situations. It is the citizen-facing paper alternative to Finland's normally prefilled/MyTax-e-filed annual tax return: the form itself instructs the filer not to enclose receipts or free-form clarifications, only to keep them for the Tax Administration to request if needed. This document describes the form only; it does not submit anything, and does not imply Finnish government endorsement.

source.documentRef: 3023e — Form 50A, "Earned income and deductions" (VEROH 3023e 1.2025), Vero's own English-language fillable AcroForm PDF

verification.notes: GOV-2308 (child of the recurring "GovSchema Standard Research" routine, GOV-2306). Closes Finland's Taxes vertical (5 of 6; only DMV remains, already confirmed weak/gated in a prior cycle). Fetched fresh with a plain HTTP GET: HTTP 200, 233,558 bytes, SHA-256 d9c1c1141be4e7ead1a464bd2ddb6ee48498b00cbf9cc1077447727ee3343608, genuine %PDF-1.7, 4 pages, no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate. A pdfjs-dist getFieldObjects() pass found 127 distinct field names across 141 field-object array entries; 6 of those entries are non-terminal placeholder nodes with page -1 and no rect (fields "8", "010", "053", "262;1", "262;2", "262;3", each an extra bookkeeping entry alongside the field's real per-page widget(s)) -- except field "8", which has zero real widgets anywhere on any page, a genuine dead/orphan AcroForm field entry contributing nothing to the form and disclosed here rather than silently mapped. Cross-referencing with page.getAnnotations()/getTextContent() across all 4 pages confirms exactly 135 real rendered Widget annotations: 115 Tx (text), 17 Btn checkbox (radioButton:false throughout; 0 Ch/choice fields), and 3 Btn push-buttons (Print, To-first-page, Reset) excluded as non-data-collecting UI chrome, leaving 132 substantive widgets. Every one of the 132 was mapped to exactly one fields[] entry via a disposable reconciliation script asserting zero unmapped and zero double-mapped widgets: 132/132 accounted for, consolidating (2 repeated-header fields -- personal identity code and tax year -- each printed as an identical widget on all 4 pages, merged into 1 logical field each, saving 6 widgets; 31 visually-split euro-box+cent-box amount pairs merged into 1 decimal number field each, saving 31 widgets; 3 same-named two-option checkbox pairs for child custody type, each merged into 1 enum field, saving 3 widgets) down to 92 fields[] entries. This form has no documents[] entries: unlike this registry's other recent Finnish specimen (fi/prh/start-up-notification-y1, which has a mandatory-enclosure appendix-form checklist), this form's own introductory text explicitly instructs the filer not to enclose any receipts or free-form clarifications, only to keep them for the Tax Administration to request if needed -- so no supporting-document checklist exists on the specimen to model. A widget-rect-vs-text-line coordinate cross-reference (this registry's established technique for out-of-order generic field names) was used to correctly pair the three 8.1 "special tax deduction" cost boxes with their checkboxes, since the raw AcroForm field-name order (071, 072, 073, 074, 097, 098) does not match visual row order -- confirmed by y-coordinate overlap that the "Illness" checkbox (071) pairs with cost box "072", "Child's developmental disorder" (097) pairs with cost box "098", and "Other reason" (073) pairs with cost box "074" (i.e. the checkbox/cost pairs interleave rather than running in strict numeric sequence). Four disclosed quirks, all cross-checked via the coordinate technique and none affecting data integrity: (1) the dead/orphan field "8" noted above; (2) the "Other earned income" type field's internal AcroForm name is the Finnish "6_Mika tulo" ("mikä tulo", "what income"), left-over Finnish text on an otherwise English-labelled form; (3) the donation-recipient-name field's internal AcroForm name is the Finnish "8.6 Lahjoituksen saajan nimi" ("name of the donation recipient"), the same kind of artifact; (4) the four child-support personal-identity-code slots are internally named inconsistently -- "086,1" (comma) for slot 1 versus "086;2"/"086;3"/"086;4" (semicolon) for slots 2-4. None of these quirks affect the field model; they are disclosed as internal-naming artifacts on the specimen, not structural facts about the process. Two mock conformance scenarios (a simple wage-earner claiming union/unemployment-fund dues; a fuller scenario covering pension income, benefits, work-related expenses, a car-benefit reduction, child support to 2 children, and one non-resident tax-at-source employment period) were run against a from-scratch interpreter script walking fields[]/crossFieldValidation[] directly: 0 errors on both, plus 4 independent mutation controls (each dropping one of the 4 statically-required fields: filerName, personalIdentityCode, taxYear, signatureDate; a fifth mutation setting carBenefitReductionPrivateUseClaimed=true while omitting the requiredWhen-gated carBenefitPrivateUseKm/carBenefitWorkRelatedUseKm; a sixth reversing an employmentPeriod crossFieldValidation date-ordering rule) each correctly raised exactly 1 error. This schema has no documents[] array, so the registry's known documents[]-requiredness conformance-checker blind spot does not apply here; this is stated explicitly rather than silently omitted. Validated clean with node tools/validate.mjs and node tools/validate-ajv.mjs, individually and as part of the full registry run.

fields.name: filerName
fields.label: Your name
fields.description: The filer's full name. Printed as a repeated header field on page 1 only.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 1/4, "Personal details and tax year": "Your name"

fields.name: personalIdentityCode
fields.label: Personal identity code
fields.description: The filer's Finnish personal identity code (henkilötunnus). Printed as a repeated header field on all 4 pages of the form; this document models it as a single logical value, not 4 separate fields, since the AcroForm field itself is one field name shared by 4 page widgets.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 1/4, "Personal details and tax year": "Personal identity code"

fields.name: taxYear
fields.label: Tax year
fields.description: The tax (assessment) year this form reports earned income and deductions for. Printed as a repeated header field on all 4 pages of the form; modelled as one logical value, per the same rationale as personalIdentityCode.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 1/4, "Personal details and tax year": "Tax year"

fields.name: childSupportChildPersonalIdCode3
fields.label: Child's personal identity code (child 3)
fields.description: One of a bounded repeating group of up to 4 child personal-identity-code slots for the children this child support total covers, per this registry's established bounded-repeating-group flattening convention. The source PDF's own AcroForm field name for slot 1 is "086,1" (comma), while slots 2-4 are "086;2"/"086;3"/"086;4" (semicolon) -- a disclosed inconsistent-punctuation naming artifact on the specimen itself (see VERIFICATION.md).
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 3/4, "9 Child support paid": "Child's personal identity code"

fields.label: Child's personal identity code (child 4)

fields.name: employmentPeriodStartDate1
fields.label: Period of employment, start date (row 1)
fields.description: One of a bounded repeating group of up to 4 employment-period slots, relevant only to the tax-at-source deduction subsection for non-resident taxpayers who are leased employees. The source prints a single "ddmmyyyy" text box per date (not separate day/month/year boxes), so no split-widget merge is needed here; this document stores the value as an ISO 8601 full-date.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 3/4, "10 International situations — Tax at source deduction": "Period of employment (ddmmyyyy–ddmmyyyy), start"

fields.name: taxPaidAbroadOnFinnishPensionAmount
fields.label: Tax paid abroad on a pension received from Finland, euro
fields.description: Amount of tax paid abroad on a Finnish-sourced pension, for crediting of tax paid abroad (reverse credit). The source PDF's own internal AcroForm field name for this widget is the bare numeral "1" (with its cent companion box named "s1") -- a terse, non-descriptive internal code, disclosed rather than left unexplained (see VERIFICATION.md). Euro/cent boxes merged into one decimal number field.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 3/4, "10 International situations — Crediting of tax paid abroad (reverse credit)": "I have paid tax abroad on a pension I have received from Finland (see the instructions for completing the form)., euro / cent"

fields.name: child1Name
fields.label: Child's name (child 1)
fields.description: One of a bounded repeating group of up to 3 children without a Finnish personal ID, per this registry's established bounded-repeating-group flattening convention. Only completable with a certificate from the child's country-of-residence authorities proving guardianship.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 4/4, "11 Custody of children outside Finland": "Child's name"

fields.name: child1CustodyType
fields.label: Type of child custody (child 1)
fields.description: Merges two mutually exclusive checkbox widgets sharing one PDF field name ("262;1"/"262;2"/"262;3" per slot, radioButton:false but same-named and mutually exclusive by construction) into one enum, per this registry's established same-named-checkbox consolidation convention.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 4/4, "11 Custody of children outside Finland": "Type of child custody: Joint custody / Sole custody"

fields.name: permanentSeparationDate
fields.label: My spouse and I have separated permanently during the tax year (date)
fields.description: A date value; the source form has no separate checkbox for this statement, only a single ddmmyyyy date box, so this document models it as one optional date field whose presence itself signals that a permanent separation occurred.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 4/4, "12 Relationship change": "My spouse and I have separated permanently during the tax year. ddmmyyyy"

fields.name: signatureDate
fields.label: Date
fields.description: The date the form is signed, part of the paper form's handwritten-signature block.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 4/4: "Date"

fields.name: signatureAndPrintedName
fields.label: Signature
fields.description: The paper form requires a handwritten signature made after printing; this field captures the accompanying printed name, matching this registry's established convention for paper-form fields that require a physical signature GovSchema cannot itself capture.
fields.sourceRef: 3023e ("50A — Earned income and deductions", VEROH 3023e 1.2025), page 4/4: "Signature"
